payload.text:
I read this as a read-only ship/no-ship review of GALLAG - I'll examine all 14 captures, the DESIGN.md contract, and the source files, then deliver a director's verdict.

Let me pull in all the evidence in parallel.
First 8 captures reviewed. Now the remaining 6 captures plus the design contract and source files.

All 14 captures and DESIGN.md reviewed. I can already see two candidate visual problems (capture 13's "ENTER YOUR NAME" text overlapping the enemy formation, and capture 14's enemy formation still visible behind the attract screen). Now let me verify against the source whether these are real render-layering bugs or intentional, and check the general render/font/sfx implementation.

Key finding confirmed in `render.ts`: `drawGame` draws the formation for ALL phases (only skips enemies with state "gone"), and `drawPhaseOverlay` draws text on top. So in `enterName` and `gameOver` phases, the live enemy formation remains visible behind the overlay text. In capture 13, "ENTER YOUR NAME" at y=96 collides with the bottom row of the formation (blue enemies at ~y=96 area) — the text is drawn over enemies, and the cyan text overlaps blue enemy sprites making "NAME" hard to read. Also in capture 14 (attract after name entry), the formation is STILL drawn behind the attract screen — enemies visibly overlap the "GALLAG" title, "OMO ARCADE 2026", and "HIGH SCORES" text. Wait — in attract phase, does the formation still exist? Let me check: capture 14 clearly shows enemy sprites scattered over the title and high-score table. That's a real visual bug: attract mode should show a clean title screen (original Galaga attract has no leftover formation). Let me check the state transitions to confirm the formation isn't cleared.

Also capture 12 (GAME OVER): enemies still visible — in the original, GAME OVER typically shows over the playfield, that's acceptable. But capture 14 attract with leftover formation overlapping text is a genuine readability problem.

Let me check update.ts, playing.ts, state.ts, hud.ts, and sfx.ts to verify phase transitions and HUD details.

Now I have the full picture. Let me verify the key visual findings against the code:

**Finding 1 (capture 14 — attract with leftover formation):** `resetForAttract` in update.ts does NOT clear `game.formation`. It resets phase/score/stage/player but keeps the formation from the just-finished game. And `drawGame` draws `game.formation.enemies` for every phase, including attract. Capture 14 confirms: enemy sprites visibly overlap the "GALLAG" title, "OMO ARCADE 2026" subtitle, and "HIGH SCORES" header. This is a real readability bug — the attract screen in any 1981 cabinet is clean. Also note the enemies are frozen (attract phase never calls `updateFormation`), so they're static leftovers, not a demo.

Wait — actually, is it possible the original Galaga attract shows a demo? Galaga's attract mode shows title + hi scores, no enemy formation flying. The DESIGN.md says "ATTRACT (title + high-score table + blinking INSERT COIN + demo starfield)" — starfield only, no formation. So the leftover formation contradicts the design contract. Confirmed BLOCKER candidate... but is it a blocker or nit? It visibly garbles the title screen text — the title "GALLAG" has red/purple sprites inside the letters, "HIGH SCORES" is partially obscured. For a cabinet-authenticity core ask, a dirty attract screen is a real defect. I'd call it BLOCKER.

**Finding 2 (capture 13 — ENTER YOUR NAME overlapping formation):** Same root cause — formation drawn behind overlay. "ENTER YOUR NAME" at y=96 collides with the bottom blue enemy row; the cyan text over blue sprites makes "NAME" genuinely hard to read (in the capture, "NAME" has a blue enemy sprite right behind/through it). Same root cause as finding 1.

**Finding 3 (capture 12 — GAME OVER over formation):** Same layering, but GAME OVER over the frozen battlefield is actually period-accurate (Galaga shows GAME OVER over the playfield). Not a defect per se. But note the formation is frozen from the moment of gameOver (updateFormation not called in gameOver phase) — acceptable.

**Finding 4 — capture 04 stage intro:** "STAGE 1 / READY!" with entrance enemies mid-flight — good. But note the bottom-right shows a red boss badge at bottom right corner near CREDIT — that's the stage badge from hud.ts (bossA sprite drawn at SCREEN.width-14, height-22). In capture 04, there's a red enemy-looking sprite at bottom right — that's the stage badge. It looks like a stray enemy. Actually it's the stage-1 badge — Galaga shows stage badges bottom-right. OK, authentic. But wait — in capture 04 the badge is at the same row as CREDIT 0? Looking at capture: bottom right shows "CREDIT 0" and above-right a red sprite. hud.ts draws badges at height-22 and CREDIT at height-9 — so badge above credit, matches. Fine.

**Finding 5 — capture 06:** score popup "50" visible, explosion particles visible, bullet streams — good. But I notice in capture 06 the popup "50" appears twice-ish (one bright "50" and a dimmer "50" to its right) — two popups at different ages, fine.

**Finding 6 — capture 08 item drop:** the capsule with letter. In the capture, the item capsule shows... a yellow "P"-like glyph? ITEM_INFO labels — let me check items.ts for labels. The capsule is drawn with `spriteCanvas("itemCapsule", {ch:"C", color})` then `drawText(info.label, x+3, y+1)`. Capsule is 11px wide, 9 tall; a 5x7 glyph at x+3,y+1 fits inside. In capture 08, the falling item shows a yellow glyph inside a ring — readable. Good. But the letter in capture 08 looks like "P" — need to check ITEM_INFO labels to see what kinds exist (twin/rapid/shield/bonus/extraLife → labels?). Let me check items.ts quickly.

**Finding 7 — capture 10 shield ring:** green ring around ship visible, pulsing alpha — good, matches DESIGN.

**Finding 8 — capture 11 death explosion:** particles at player position, one life icon left (was 2), score 100149, HIGH SCORE updated live to 100149 — good. Explosion reads as multi-color shards (white/yellow/red) — matches explosion tokens.

**Finding 9 — capture 09 twin shot:** two parallel yellow bullets — good. Player mid-blink ON-phase per context note... wait, context says 09/10 intentionally show player mid-blink ON-phase. In 09 the player IS visible. ON-phase = visible. OK.

**Finding 10 — capture 07 dive:** purple enemy diving with red enemy bullets below it, player bullets in flight — dive state reads clearly. Good.

**Finding 11 — attract blink:** 01 vs 02 — INSERT COIN present in 01, absent in 02. Confirmed blink works. Also note in 01/02/03 the attract screen is CLEAN (no formation) — because at boot the formation is empty. So the dirty attract only happens after a game ends (capture 14). This strengthens finding 1: the attract screen is correct on boot but polluted after name entry — an inconsistent state reset.

**Finding 12 — capture 03 (credit):** shows "CREDIT 0"?? Wait — capture 03 is named "credit" — after inserting a coin, credits should be 1. Looking at capture 03: bottom right shows "CREDIT 0"... Hmm, let me look again. Capture 03 shows the attract screen with INSERT COIN visible and CREDIT 0 at bottom. But the scenario name is "03-credit" — presumably after pressing coin. If a coin was inserted, attract should show "PRESS ENTER TO START" (per drawAttract: credits>0 → PRESS ENTER TO START) and CREDIT 1. Capture 03 shows INSERT COIN and CREDIT 0. Hmm — but maybe the capture was taken at the moment before the coin press, or the scenario pressed coin and then the blink phase shows INSERT COIN... no — if credits>0, INSERT COIN is never drawn; it draws PRESS ENTER TO START (blinking). Capture 03 clearly shows "INSERT COIN" in yellow and "CREDIT 0". That suggests the coin insert didn't register at capture time, OR the capture is the "before" frame. The context says 4/4 scenarios pass, so presumably the scenario asserts something. I shouldn't over-claim: maybe the capture timing caught it pre-coin. But wait — the file is named 03-credit and the task says captures drove the real game with scenarios passing. If the scenario asserted CREDIT 1 and the capture shows CREDIT 0, that'd be a failed assertion — so likely the capture is intentionally the attract-with-insert-coin frame. I can't verify the scenario; I'll not flag this as a bug but note ambiguity... Actually, let me check the qa.ts / test to see what scenario 03 does. Let me look at qa.ts and the e2e tests.

Also **capture 04**: "CREDIT 0" after starting — correct (credit consumed at start). Good.

**Finding 13 — HUD lives:** capture 04/05 show 2 ship icons bottom-left (lives-1 = 2, so 3 lives total). Galaga shows remaining ships including... original shows reserve ships. Fine.

**Finding 14 — high score live update:** capture 11/12 show HIGH SCORE 100149 tracking 1UP — hud.ts uses max(best, score) — good arcade behavior.

**Finding 15 — capture 13 name entry:** "AAA" big letters with cursor underline under first A — visible. "ARROWS CHANGE FIRE OK" — good. But the overlap issue (finding 2).

**Finding 16 — DESIGN.md consistency:** blink cadence 530ms — check TIMING.blinkMs in config. Let me check config.ts. Also scanlines/vignette in style.css. Let me read style.css and config.ts, plus items.ts for labels, and the qa/test files to understand capture 03.

Also check: DESIGN says "hit flash ≤ 2 frames" — drawEnemy hitFlash uses `enemy.hitFlashUntilMs` — set where? In collisions presumably ~100ms. Fine.

One more check — **capture 05 formation**: top row 4 red bosses, then 8 purple, then 8+8 blue = 28 enemies. Galaga-esque. Formation looks clean, sprites distinct. The boss sprites in capture 05 look red with yellow trim — good. Note bosses in the attract SCORE ADVANCE TABLE (capture 01): the boss sprite shown is red/yellow, mid purple, low blue — matches.

**Capture 01 vs 02**: also the whole attract is identical otherwise — good.

**Marquee**: cyan/red glow "GALLAG" — matches DESIGN (system sans, letter-spaced, cyan/red glow). Cabinet: dark room, cabinet body, bezel, screen with rounded corners. Coin-slot panel at bottom: "● 1 COIN · 1 PLAY   ARROWS MOVE · Z FIRE · ENTER COIN" — present in all captures. Good. Scanlines: visible on close look? The captures show subtle scanline texture on the title letters (the big GALLAG title shows horizontal striping). Yes — visible in the big title glyphs. Vignette: corners dark. Good.

Missing cabinet conventions: no "© 1981 NAMCO" style copyright line (they have OMO ARCADE 2026 — fine), no 2UP/2-player alternating play (brief didn't demand), no demo gameplay in attract (Galaga actually doesn't have demo gameplay either; it shows title + scores + score advance table — matches!). Actually Galaga's attract does cycle: title, hi-score, score advance table... here it's one static screen with all — acceptable.

What about the **stage badge row overlapping CREDIT** when stage > 4? badges up to 8 at 11px spacing from right edge: 8 badges × 11px = 88px wide starting at width-14. CREDIT text at width-52. Badges at y=height-22, CREDIT at y=height-9 — different rows, no collision. But 8 badges would extend leftward to width-14-77 = width-91, still right of center. OK.

**Lives icons vs stage badges**: lives bottom-left, badges bottom-right — fine.

Now let me check config.ts for blinkMs=530, and items.ts labels, and the qa/e2e capture scenario for 03. Also style.css for scanlines/CRT.

Now let me check the QA capture scenario to understand capture 03 (credit) and confirm the capture conditions, plus check where hitFlashUntilMs is set and whether the formation is frozen in attract (already confirmed: attract phase in updateGame never calls updateFormation).

Now I understand capture 03: it's taken right after `credits === 1` is confirmed. But the capture shows "CREDIT 0"... wait, let me re-examine capture 03. Looking again at the third image: bottom right reads "CREDIT 0". Hmm — but the waitState asserted credits === 1 before the shot. However — `drawHud` is NOT called in attract phase (`if (game.phase.kind !== "attract") drawHud(...)`). So where does "CREDIT 0" come from in attract captures? It doesn't — the attract screen has no CREDIT text. Let me look again at capture 03's bottom-right... In capture 03 I see "CREDIT 0" — but wait, that can't be drawn in attract. Let me zoom into that region to be sure rather than speculate.

The zoomed capture 14 confirms it decisively: the leftover enemy formation is drawn over/behind the attract screen — red bosses sit inside the "GALLAG" title letters, purple and blue enemies march across "OMO ARCADE 2026", and blue enemies completely obscure "HIGH SC[ORE]S" and overlap the score table ("30000" has an enemy on it). This is a real, severe readability bug with a confirmed root cause: `resetForAttract` (update.ts) never clears `game.formation`, and `drawGame` (render.ts) draws the formation in every phase including `attract`.

My crop of 03's bottom-right was off — it grabbed black screen area. The "CREDIT 0" I thought I saw in capture 03 doesn't exist there (attract doesn't draw HUD). Let me re-crop the correct bottom-right bezel area of capture 03 and also capture 13's mid region to verify the name-entry overlap.

Both findings now confirmed with zoomed evidence:

1. **Capture 03**: The attract screen after 1 credit shows "INSERT COIN" missing and... wait — this capture shows neither INSERT COIN nor PRESS ENTER TO START. The blink window caught the OFF phase. But which prompt? Per drawAttract, with credits=1 it blinks "PRESS ENTER TO START". The capture caught the off-blink, so we can't tell from this frame — fine, no defect here. The attract screen is clean (no formation) because at boot the formation is empty. Also note: no CREDIT counter visible in attract (HUD not drawn in attract) — so after inserting a coin, the player gets zero visible confirmation of their credit except a blink-dependent prompt. In a real cabinet, CREDIT 1 is always visible. That's a genuine nit: credit count is invisible in attract mode.

2. **Capture 13 zoomed**: "ENTER YOUR NAME" — the word "NAME" has a blue enemy sprite directly behind/through its glyphs, and another blue enemy sits right after the "E". The cyan text over blue sprites materially hurts readability. Same root cause (formation drawn during enterName, and enterName text placed at y=96 which is inside the formation's vertical band). Also a blue enemy sits right above the "AAA" letters.

Both stem from one root cause: the formation is never cleared/hidden when leaving play. Also confirmed in code: attract phase never calls `updateFormation`, so those enemies are frozen static — not a "demo", just debris.

One more check — the `enterName` overlay at y=96 vs formation originY=46 + 4 rows × 18px = 46..118. So the formation bottom row is at ~y=100-118, exactly where "ENTER YOUR NAME" (y=96, 7px tall → 96-103) sits. Guaranteed collision whenever the bottom rows survive. And in attract, the formation occupies y=46-118, colliding with title (y=34-62 at scale 4 → 34+28=62), subtitle (74), HIGH SCORES (96), and table rows (108-148). Guaranteed collision. These aren't unlucky frames — they're deterministic layout collisions.

Let me also double-check the "PRESS ENTER TO START" vs capture 03 and verify there's no CREDIT display in attract by re-checking drawAttract — confirmed above: no credit line in attract. And check main.ts quickly for the CRT/scanline wiring and whether the attract starfield drifts (DESIGN says attract drift = cabinet alive). Also verify the font test / sfx test exist (they do per tests listing). I have enough. One last look at main.ts for completeness of wiring (mute, coin).

Wiring confirmed: starfield updates every frame in all phases (attract drift alive), CRT overlay + marquee + coin panel in index.html, scanlines/RGB grille/vignette/flicker in style.css. Everything checks out except the formation-layering bug and the invisible-credit nit.

Review complete. Here is my verdict as the Meticulous Game Director.

---

VERDICT: REQUEST_CHANGES
CONFIDENCE: HIGH

SUMMARY: The cabinet illusion is genuinely strong — marquee, bezel, scanlines, vignette, blinking INSERT COIN, score-advance table, the works — and the in-game action reads beautifully frame to frame. But the attract screen and the name-entry screen are both vandalized by a leftover enemy formation that should have been cleared when the game ended: enemies sit inside the GALLAG title letters, march across OMO ARCADE 2026, and chew through the HIGH SCORES header and the word NAME. On a real 1981 cabinet the attract screen is sacred, spotless real estate — this one looks like the monitor has burn-in. One root cause, two broken screens. Fix it and this ships.

FINDINGS:

1. [BLOCKER] [14-hiscore-updated.png + src/game/update.ts `resetForAttract` + src/game/render.ts `drawGame`] The attract screen after a completed game is drawn with the previous game's frozen enemy formation still on screen. Zoomed evidence: red boss sprites sit inside the GALLAG title glyphs, purple/blue enemies cross "OMO ARCADE 2026", and blue enemies fully obscure "HIGH SC[ORE]S" and overlap the "30000" score row. Root cause is confirmed in code: `resetForAttract` resets phase/score/stage/player but never clears `game.formation`, and `drawGame` unconditionally draws `game.formation.enemies` in every phase, including attract. The attract-phase update loop also never calls `updateFormation`, so these are static debris, not a demo — they will never move. This is a deterministic layout collision, not an unlucky frame: formation rows live at y≈46–118, exactly where the title (y=34), subtitle (y=74), HIGH SCORES header (y=96), and table rows (y=108–148) are drawn. It also contradicts DESIGN.md §6, which defines ATTRACT as "title + high-score table + blinking INSERT COIN + demo starfield" — starfield only, no formation. DEMANDED FIX: clear (or skip drawing) the formation when entering attract — e.g. reset `formation` in `resetForAttract`, or gate the enemy draw loop on non-attract phases. Note the boot-time attract (captures 01/02) is clean only because the formation starts empty; the bug only surfaces after a game ends, which is precisely the moment a returning player sees the cabinet.

2. [BLOCKER] [13-enter-name.png + src/game/render.ts `drawPhaseOverlay` enterName branch] Same root cause, second victim: "ENTER YOUR NAME" is drawn at y=96, directly inside the surviving formation's bottom row. In the zoomed capture, a blue enemy sprite sits behind the glyphs of "NAME" and another sits immediately after the "E"; a third blue enemy hovers just above the giant "AAA" initials. Cyan 5x7 text over blue animated sprites is genuinely hard to read — this is the player's moment of glory after a hi-score and it looks broken. DEMANDED FIX: once the formation is cleared/hidden outside play (fix #1), this screen resolves with it; if any enemies are intentionally kept visible during name entry, move the prompt below the formation band (y≥130) instead.

3. [NIT] [03-credit.png + src/game/render.ts `drawAttract` + src/hud.ts] The credit count is invisible in attract mode. `drawHud` (which draws "CREDIT n") is skipped in attract, and `drawAttract` draws no credit line — so after inserting a coin, the only confirmation is the blinking "PRESS ENTER TO START", which is absent entirely during the off-blink half of every 530ms cycle (capture 03 caught exactly such an off-frame: no prompt, no credit indicator, nothing). Every 1981 cabinet shows CREDIT 1 the instant the coin drops. DEMANDED FIX: draw a persistent "CREDIT 1" line in attract when credits > 0 (bottom corner, matching the in-game HUD position).

4. [NIT] [12-game-over.png] GAME OVER is drawn over the frozen battlefield with a diving enemy parked mid-screen. This is period-plausible (the original overlays GAME OVER on the playfield), so I am not blocking on it — but once fix #1 lands, verify GAME OVER still shows the final formation, because that one is the screen where the leftover formation is actually correct.

WHAT IS GOOD (must not regress):
- Cabinet authenticity: marquee with cyan/red offset glow, rounded CRT bezel with inner shadow, 2px scanlines + faint RGB aperture grille + corner vignette + subtle flicker, and the coin-slot panel with pulsing yellow lamp and "1 COIN · 1 PLAY" legend — all present in every capture and matching DESIGN.md tokens.
- Attract conventions: title, OMO ARCADE 2026, 5-entry hi-score table, SCORE ADVANCE TABLE with the three enemy sprites and point values, blinking INSERT COIN verified across 01/02 opposite blink frames at the contract's 530ms cadence (TIMING.blinkMs = 530).
- Sprites: player ship with white cockpit spine and flickering two-frame engine flame; three enemy families (red/yellow boss, purple mid, blue low) each with two-frame leg animation — all crisp, distinct, and readable at 224x288. No malformed sprites found in any capture.
- Game-feel readability: multi-shard explosions with hot/mid/cool palette (06, 11), floating "50" score popups with fade (06), twin-shot as two parallel yellow bolts (09), pulsing green shield ring (10), diving enemy with red enemy bullet spray clearly signaled (07), death sequence with screen shake + red flash + double explosion (11). Every effect signals its state per the motion-means-state rule.
- HUD/flow: 1UP red label + score, HIGH SCORE blue label with live max(best, score) tracking (visible climbing to 100149 in 11/12), lives as ship icons bottom-left, stage badges as mini-boss icons bottom-right, CREDIT counter in play, STAGE 1 / READY! banner (04), GAME OVER in accent red (12), name entry with 3-letter initials and blinking cursor underline (13), hi-score table correctly updated with BAA 100149 at rank 1 (14). No clipped text or misalignment anywhere in the play HUD.
- Sound (judged from score data and wiring, not heard): SFX_SCORES are real melodic chiptune phrases — 5-note stage jingle, 4-note item arpeggio, two-tone coin ping, descending 3-note game-over, swept saw player-death with noise — not bare blips, and all eight events are wired to game events in update.ts/playing.ts including dive swoosh and shield-absorb. Matches DESIGN.md §5.
- Nothing faked: sprites are procedural pixel art from a validated glyph/row table (SpriteDefError on ragged rows), the font is a real 5x7 bitmap table, captures were driven through the real state machine — no static mock-ups detected.

BLOCKERS:
1. Clear or hide the enemy formation when entering attract mode — the post-game attract screen (14) has frozen enemies inside the title, subtitle, HIGH SCORES header, and score table. Fix in `resetForAttract` (update.ts) and/or the draw gate in `drawGame` (render.ts).
2. Same fix must clear the name-entry screen (13): "ENTER YOUR NAME" at y=96 currently renders on top of the surviving bottom enemy row, with a sprite visibly behind the word "NAME". If any formation is deliberately kept for that screen, move the prompt below y≈130 instead.
